description: High-end perimeter defense and persistent aerial overwatch. Integrate tethered drone systems, thermal anomaly detection, and unified target tracking across VMS platforms.
learning_objectives: Understand the threat landscape: rogue drone categories, payload risks, and airspace vulnerabilities
Build AI models for drone detection, classification, and intent inference using multi‑sensor fusion
Design real‑time geospatial data pipelines for multi‑sensor C‑UAS systems
Deploy low‑latency detection and tracking models on edge hardware for rapid response
Implement pursuit‑evasion algorithms and safe intercept planning
Operate within legal and regulatory frameworks for C‑UAS deployments
Continuously retrain models as adversarial drone designs evolve

modules.title: Persistent Tethered Overwatch Operations
modules.description: Deploy micro-tether systems providing unjammable, continuous power and high-bandwidth fiber optic data streams.
topics: Tether tension control and winching algorithms
Gimbal stabilization for high-zoom lenses at high altitudes
Integration with VMS (Video Management Systems) via ONVIF/RTSP

modules.title: Multi-Spectral Intrusion Detection
modules.description: Utilize edge computing to run state-of-the-art thermal target detection and cross-sensor tracking.
topics: Long-Wave Infrared (LWIR) vs Mid-Wave Infrared (MWIR) sensors

capstone_project.name: Autonomous C‑UAS Sentinel System
capstone_project.description: Build an integrated counter‑drone system that uses radar micro‑Doppler signatures and RF fingerprinting to detect and classify rogue drones within a 1 km radius, tracks them with a visual interceptor drone, and logs all incidents as STAC items for evidence and model improvement. The system must detect a DJI Phantom‑class drone in <3 seconds from first radar return and maintain track through evasive maneuvers.
deliverables: Trained radar micro‑Doppler CNN classifier (>95% TPR at <1% FPR)
RF fingerprinting model with siamese network architecture
ROS 2 pursuit‑evasion node with proportional navigation
Compliance checklist for C‑UAS deployment in target jurisdiction

resources.name: DroneShield C‑UAS
resources.description: Commercial C‑UAS systems and sensor integration reference

resources.name: FAA C‑UAS Resources
resources.description: Legal framework and authorized C‑UAS deployment guidance

resources.name: AUVSI C‑UAS White Paper
resources.description: Industry guidance on counter‑UAS technology and standards

glossary.definition: An open industry forum that provides and promotes standardized interfaces for effective interoperability of IP-based physical security products.
Long-Wave Infrared. Typically refers to the 8 to 14 micrometer spectrum, optimal for detecting body heat temperatures.
